title: Henckelia urticifolia
intro: Henckelia urticifolia, also known as the stinging passion flower, is a perennial flowering plant native to tropical Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as wound healing, skin infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Henckelia urticifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Henckelia urticifolia is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Henckelia urticifolia is a species of plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This plant is native to certain regions in Asia, including Southeast Asia and China. The Gesneriaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes over 3,000 species. Many plants within this family are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Classification of plants in the Gesneriaceae family is often based on characteristics such as leaf shape, flower color, and growth habit.
uses: Henckelia urticifolia, a plant native to Southeast Asia, has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. Its leaves and stems are valued for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, Henckelia urticifolia is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant is also used to treat wounds and skin infections due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Henckelia urticifolia for medicinal use.

parts: Henckelia urticifolia, the medicinal parts of this plant are primarily its roots and leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
